div.p: Color Key: 🟣 Header/Input 🔵 Message bytes ⬜ Zero fill 🟠 Length field 🔴 Boundary trap

g.text: Case 3: 55-byte message (len=55, 440 bits) ← LAST single-block case
Case 4: 56-byte message (len=56, 448 bits) ← THE BOUNDARY TRAP

div.p: Bytes 1..55 = 0x00 → 55 zero bytes (memset handles this via initial memset of entire struct)
Bytes 56..55 = 0 zero bytes (marker lands exactly at byte 55) Length field goes immediately at byte 56
[00..54] 0xAA × 55 ← message bytes [55] 0x80 ← marker (0 zero-fill bytes after!) [56..63] 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 0xB8 num_blocks = 1
Block 0 tail: bytes 57..63 = 0x00 × 7 Block 1 body: bytes 64..119 = 0x00 × 56 Total zero fill = 63 bytes
used=57 > 56 ⟹ two blocks required! One extra byte (56→57) triggers an entirely new block. 55 bytes → 1 block. 56 bytes → 2 blocks. This is the #1 first-attempt implementation error.
General formula: total_padded = ((used + 8 + 63) / 64) * 64 where used = len + 1 Boundary condition: if used > 56 → 2 blocks if used ≤ 56 → 1 block Common mistakes caught by these 4 tests:

ol.li: Little-endian memcpy for length field → wrong hash everywhere
bit_length = len instead of len * 8 → length field off by ×8
